title: Tender Mustardy French Beef Choux Puffs
description: A French-inspired dish featuring tender beef and mustardy flavors in a choux puff, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is sugar-free-friendly and uses a fondue pot as the cooking vessel.
instructions: Preheat the fondue pot to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the choux pastry dough to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
Cut out small squares of dough, about 3 inches per side.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and cook the beef strips until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the Dijon mustard and vanilla extract to the skillet and stir to combine.
Spoon a small amount of the beef mixture onto one half of each dough square.
Fold the other half of the dough square over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Carefully place the choux puffs into the preheated fondue pot, being careful not to overcrowd.
Cook the choux puffs for 10-12 minutes, or until golden brown, turning occasionally.
Serve the choux puffs hot, garnished with grated cheese, chopped parsley, and a side of chicken broth for dipping.
